intentional_test_features: Right-heart congestion cluster on a background of severe COPD: raised JVP, massive bilateral oedema, tender hepatomegaly and ascites signal decompensated cor pulmonale that must be recognised as urgent, not routine COPD grumbling
Resting hypoxia (SpO2 89% on room air) with tachypnoea and accessory-muscle use must be treated as a serious decompensation, not baseline COPD
Anchoring trap: bilateral leg swelling plus known lung disease can be dismissed as 'chronic'; the new ascites, hepatomegaly and rising oedema mark acute right-heart decompensation
Differentiation trap: must distinguish right-sided (cor pulmonale) from left-sided heart failure, DVT and cirrhosis - the systemic venous congestion pattern points central and right-sided
Oxygen-titration caution: in COPD, uncontrolled high-flow oxygen risks CO2 retention; telehealth cannot titrate safely, reinforcing the need for in-person assessment

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
